title: Command Lifecycle, Main Boundary, and Decomposition · Language Clinic: Function Contracts
summary: A one-file script reads global arguments during import, mixes file access with counting and printing, cannot be tested without exiting, and changes behavior when imported. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the function contracts increment in testable command lifecycle and function map. Learners progress throug…
objectives: Decompose admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, and orchestration into explicit contracts.
Keep import-safe definitions separate from the __main__ execution boundary and return an exit status from main.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: bookbot-function-contracts — Decompose admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, and orchestration into explicit contracts.
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-cli-lifecycle-contract-3
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-cli-lifecycle-contract-3.
# Case 5798: a large-file migration rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: retain source permission, digest purpose, revision, Python, Unicode, policy, command, and output identity.
# Changed case: split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte.
def worked_bookbot_cli_lifecycle_contract_3(import_safe: bool = True, pure_stages: int = 7, side_effect_boundaries: int = 2, hidden_globals: int = 0, exit_status: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "corpusworked3",
        "policy3dekrow",
        "revision5798",
        "commandworked35798",
        "report3dekrow5798",
        "test5798worked3",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if not import_safe or pure_stages < 1:
        return False, pure_stages, "lifecycle"
    if side_effect_boundaries < 1 or side_effect_boundaries > 3:
        return False, side_effect_boundaries, "effect-shell"
    if hidden_globals > 0:
        return False, hidden_globals, "hidden-state"
    if exit_status != 0:
        return False, exit_status, "command-failure"
    assert pure_stages > side_effect_boundaries
    return True, pure_stages, "testable-command"

    evidence_axes_1l91lj = (
        "corpus1l91lj",
        "policyjl19l1",
        "revision2316",
        "command1l91lj2316",
        "reportjl19l12316",
        "test23161l91lj",

    if side_effect_boundaries < 1 or side_effect_boundaries > 3:

    if hidden_globals > 0:

    if exit_status != 0:

    return True, pure_stages, "testable-command"

For Bookbot and Python Defend the Function Contracts model · Theory Function Contracts, keep Function Contracts connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Decompose admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, and orchestration into explicit contracts.

hints: For Bookbot and Python Defend the Function Contracts model · Theory Function Contracts, name the stakeholder, corpus authority, revision, path, bytes, encoding, Unicode, token, count, rank, report, command, test, package, release, and transfer boundary, Function Contracts precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Bookbot and Python Defend the Function Contracts model · Theory Function Contracts, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, stdout, stderr, status, test, package, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory Bookbot team handles case 3942 during a digital-humanities corpus revision. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate import-safe definitions, a pure analysis core, explicit effects, and command status; test empty, malformed, Unicode, tied, large, changed-policy, and rejected cases before accepting output; then install the built wheel in a fresh environment and rerun success, invalid-argument, and decode-failure cases. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Decompose admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, and orchestration into explicit contracts.

For Bookbot and Python Defend the Function Contracts model · Theory Function Contracts, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: More tiny functions automatically create a better design.
